description.summary: A Japanese yokai that appears in unclean bathrooms at night, depicted as a small humanoid creature with a long tongue that licks filth, grime, and mold from dirty surfaces. Serves as a cautionary tale promoting cleanliness.
description.full: Akaname, whose name literally means 'filth licker,' is a distinctive yokai from Japanese folklore that inhabits dirty, neglected bathrooms. This small humanoid creature appears at night in bathrooms that have been left unclean, using its characteristically long tongue to lick filth, grime, mold, and accumulated dirt from walls, floors, and fixtures. Depicted with grimy skin, disheveled hair, and a generally grotesque appearance, Akaname is nonetheless harmless to humans—its presence serves more as a consequence and reminder of poor hygiene than as a genuine threat. The yokai embodies a practical folk belief designed to encourage cleanliness and proper maintenance of bathrooms, particularly traditional Japanese baths. In folklore, the simple prevention is maintaining cleanliness—Akaname will not appear in a well-kept bathroom. In modern times, the yokai has been featured in cleaning product advertising and hygiene education, demonstrating how traditional folklore adapts to serve contemporary purposes. Akaname represents the Japanese cultural emphasis on cleanliness and the consequences of negligence, packaged in the form of a disgusting but ultimately harmless supernatural being.

etymology.origin: 'Aka' means filth, grime, or scum, and 'name/nameru' means to lick.

related_phenomena.name: Kappa
related_phenomena.description: Water yokai, both associated with water/bathing areas

features.description: Extremely long tongue for licking filth
Small humanoid with dirty, unkempt look

physical.morphology: Small humanoid creature with grotesque, dirty appearance and disproportionately long tongue.

abilities.description: Licks and consumes bathroom grime, mold, and filth
Manifests only at night in dirty bathrooms
Only appears in neglected, dirty bathrooms

patterns: Licks dirty surfaces with long tongue
Serves as indicator of poor hygiene

range.description: Traditional Japanese folklore, particularly associated with household maintenance and cleanliness traditions.

peak_activity_periods.description: Cautionary tales for maintaining bathroom cleanliness
Revival in advertising and hygiene education

origins.cultural_roots: Japanese domestic folklore emphasizing cleanliness and proper household maintenance, particularly of bathrooms and bathing areas which hold cultural significance.
origins.folklore_connections: Part of broader Japanese tradition of yokai that enforce social norms and proper behavior through cautionary tales.

timeline.significance: Stories used to encourage bathroom cleanliness
Featured in cleaning product advertising and hygiene education

history.modern_era: Akaname continues to appear in Japanese popular culture, yokai collections, and has been adapted for modern hygiene education and cleaning product marketing, demonstrating the practical application of folklore.

stories: Children warned to keep bathrooms clean
Disgusting creature as motivation for hygiene
folklore.beliefs: Cleanliness prevents supernatural intrusion. Negligence attracts unwanted spirits. Proper household maintenance is both practical and spiritual duty.

indigenous.significance: Reflects Japanese cultural emphasis on cleanliness, particularly in bathing areas. Bathrooms and baths hold special significance in Japanese culture.
indigenous.sensitivity_note: Akaname folklore reflects important Japanese cultural values regarding cleanliness and household maintenance.
indigenous.respect_guidelines: Understand the cultural context of cleanliness in Japanese society and the practical purpose of such cautionary yokai.

researchers.name: Shigeru Mizuki
researchers.contribution: Modern documentation of yokai folklore including domestic spirits

organizations.name: International Research Center for Japanese Studies

sources.title: The Book of Yokai: Mysterious Creatures of Japanese Folklore
